description: Build a Streamlit app that lets KA supervisors upload a store list, cluster stores into geographic daily beats per field agent, enforce GMV-based visit priority, visualize beats on a map, assign days, and export per-agent CSV schedules.

userStories.title: Project scaffold + password gate
userStories.description: Scaffold the Streamlit app with a password gate and three placeholder sections: Upload, Beat Planner, Export.
acceptanceCriteria: app.py runs with `streamlit run app.py` without errors
requirements.txt includes streamlit, pandas, openpyxl, scikit-learn, folium, streamlit-folium, numpy
.streamlit/config.toml sets a clean blue theme
Password input shown on load; wrong password blocks access
Correct password (ka2026) reveals main app shell with three labelled sections
lib/ directory exists with empty __init__.py
GitHub issue #2 closed with comment linking to the commit

userStories.title: XLSX parser + agent classifier
userStories.description: XLSX upload UI and parser. Validates required columns, classifies agents as field or caller, surfaces a summary.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/parser.py exports parse_xlsx(file) -> dict with keys: stores (DataFrame), field_agents (list), caller_agents (list), errors (list)
Required columns validated: store_id, store_name, agent, gcu, warehouse, point_y, point_x, visit_day, cohort, MTD Delivered, Last Delivered Order Date
Missing columns returned in errors; app shows st.error with missing column names
Field agents detected by prefix [SKAS/ or [GKAS/; caller agents by prefix [Caller/KA]
point_y mapped to lat, point_x mapped to lng internally
All 3 cohort values pass through: Active But High GMV Store, Active But Low GMV Store, Inactive Store
After upload, app shows: total stores, field agent count, caller agent count, store count per field agent
GitHub issue #3 closed with comment linking to the commit

userStories.title: Beat clusterer + P1/P2 resolver
userStories.description: K-means geographic clustering, cluster-to-agent assignment, and P1/P2 outlier classification.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/clustering.py exports run_clustering(stores_df, beat_size, field_agents) -> dict with keys: beats (list of dicts), p2_stores (DataFrame)
Each beat dict has: beat_id, assigned_agent, stores (DataFrame)
k = ceil(total_stores / beat_size); scikit-learn KMeans with init=k-means++
Clusters smaller than beat_size * 0.5 merged into nearest neighbor before agent assignment
Each cluster assigned to nearest field agent by centroid distance
P2 stores: distance to assigned cluster centroid exceeds mean inter-centroid distance
P2 stores removed from beats and returned separately
Supervisor sets beat size via st.number_input (min 5, max 100, default 30); re-run without re-upload
App shows beat count per field agent and P2 store count
GitHub issue #4 closed with comment linking to the commit

userStories.title: Beat composer — GMV priority + visit frequency
userStories.description: Post-clustering step: enforces GMV priority within beats, trims over-capacity, duplicates stores into multiple day-slots.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/composer.py exports compose_beats(beats, beat_size) -> list of ComposedBeat dicts
Within each beat, stores sorted: Active But High GMV Store first, Active But Low GMV Store second, Inactive Store last
If beat exceeds beat_size: Inactive trimmed first, then Low GMV; High GMV never trimmed
Active But High GMV stores duplicated into 3 day-slots; Low GMV into 2; Inactive into 1
Each slot entry includes store with slot_index (1, 2, or 3) and originating beat_id
GitHub issue #5 closed with comment linking to the commit

userStories.title: Beat map (folium)
userStories.description: Interactive Folium map: beats color-coded by field agent, P2 stores in grey, store popups with GMV info.
acceptanceCriteria: Map rendered via streamlit-folium (st_folium)
Each field agent assigned a distinct color; all beats for that agent share it
Store circle markers; popup shows: store name, agent, beat ID, cohort, MTD Delivered, Last Delivered Date
P2 stores shown as grey markers with caller assignment in popup
Map auto-fits bounds to all store coordinates
Map re-renders when clustering is re-run
GitHub issue #6 closed with comment linking to the commit

userStories.title: Day assignment + beat reassignment UI
userStories.description: UI for assigning days (Mon–Sat) to beats and reassigning beats between agents. Shows agent × day summary grid.
acceptanceCriteria: Each beat shown in table row: beat ID, assigned agent (editable dropdown), store count, day selector (Mon–Sat + Unassigned)
Beats grouped by agent
Changing agent dropdown reassigns that beat in session state
Changing day selector updates beat's day assignment in session state
Summary table shows agent × day grid with store count per cell
Unassigned beats visually highlighted
GitHub issue #7 closed with comment linking to the commit

userStories.title: CSV export — field agents + P2 callers
userStories.description: Two CSV download buttons: per-agent-per-day schedule for field agents, flat list for P2 caller stores.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/exporter.py exports build_field_csv(beats, day_assignments) -> bytes and build_caller_csv(p2_stores) -> bytes
Field CSV columns: agent_name, day, beat_id, store_id, store_name, gcu, city, locality, cohort, mtd_delivered, last_delivered_date
Field CSV sorted: agent_name -> day (Mon first) -> beat_id -> store_id
Caller CSV columns: agent_name, store_id, store_name, gcu, city, locality, cohort, mtd_delivered, last_delivered_date
Both via st.download_button — no server-side file writing
Buttons disabled if any beat is still Unassigned
File names: ka_field_schedule_<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv and ka_caller_list_<YYYY-MM-DD>.csv
GitHub issue #8 closed with comment linking to the commit
